title: Bad Bunny's Grammy Win Drives Massive Streaming Surge
text: Bad Bunny made history at the 2026 Grammy Awards when Debí Tirar Más Fotos became "the first Spanish-language album to be awarded Album of the Year," according to Rolling Stone. The album's win triggered an immediate audience boost. Data from Luminate showed that streams of Debí Tirar Más Fotos "increased by 240 percent on Monday compared to the prior Monday," with the album garnering over 16 million on-demand streams in the United States alone. Across all his music, Bad Bunny accumulated 36 million total streams within 24 hours following his victory—a 117 percent increase from typical numbers. Digital singles sales jumped dramatically, with those from the album rising 903 percent to approximately 2,000 sales. A memorable moment during the ceremony also boosted engagement. When "Grammy Awards host Trevor Noah serenaded Bad Bunny with the English-translation of his song" from the album, that track saw a 273 percent streaming increase compared to the previous week. Bad Bunny is poised to generate additional streaming gains this weekend when he becomes the first artist to headline the Super Bowl halftime show performing entirely in Spanish.
